objective: Prototype the smallest online matrix-handoff step from CPU motion estimation toward the accepted MMAPI/VPI/NVENC device-side warp and encode path.

accepted_stage_dependency.claim_boundary: Accepted only as offline device-side warp/encode stage demo; not full real-time EIS.

allowed: frame-index alignment and matrix handoff timing are measured
quality no-regression is checked against accepted CPU/device evidence

first_implementation_slice: Use the accepted 120-row post_geometry_identity_first matrix CSV as the control stream.
Build a mock online handoff that provides the same matrices with frame-index timestamps.
Verify the handoff output matches the CSV replay path within expected encoding variance.
Only after that, replace mock matrices with live CPU-estimated matrices.
frozen_variables: CPU baseline and accepted device-stage evidence
success_criteria: frame-index alignment log has no gaps
VPI warp avg remains near the current 1.44 ms/frame order
quality does not regress relative to accepted post_geometry_identity_first stage output

recovery_actions.matrix_frame_mismatch_after_one_fix_atte: Stop and inspect frame-index ownership before changing algorithm or VPI code.
recovery_actions.output_empty_or_unreadable: Check MMAPI command shape and avoid --stats on the current matrix transcode path.
recovery_actions.broad_cxx_rewrite_needed: Return to design review; do not rewrite the MMAPI sample without a smaller verifier.
